title: Verified connections ledger
description: The dashboard's connect-the-dots layer: edges between facts already published elsewhere on the site. Every connection MUST carry a connectionTier (the cross-document link confidence, distinct from each source's own tier) so an unverified link cannot be published untiered. Only status:published renders in the UI.

reviewed.description: YYYY-MM — when a curator last re-verified this ledger against sources. Drives the staleness gate.

kind.description: two_sider = one deal in two parties' documents; thread = one player's moves over time; event_metric = a news event that moved a filed number; contradiction = two true facts in tension; circular = a financing/supply loop; supersedes = a newer fact replacing an older one; dependency = one layer's output paces another's (an upstream bottleneck).

insight.description: The connect-the-dots read a CSO could not get from any single Google search. States what is known vs inferred.

connectionTier.description: REQUIRED, no default. both_sides = both parties on record; one_side = only one party names the link; inference = analyst framing over sourced facts. The badge is the guardrail — a connection cannot render without it.

namedBy.description: Asymmetry disclosure: who names the counterparty. Required in spirit whenever connectionTier is one_side.

date.description: When the connection became visible (for the weekly surface).

status.description: Only 'published' (and explicitly-flagged 'downgraded') render in the UI.

side.description: buyer / seller / '' — which end of a two-sided deal this fact anchors.

label.description: The published fact this edge rests on — must already appear elsewhere on the dashboard.

src.description: Source label (linkified against sources.json where known).

srcTier.description: The SOURCE's own tier — distinct from connectionTier.

href.description: Deep-link to where the fact lives on the dashboard (e.g. #capital:overcommitmentBoard) or an external primary URL (EDGAR).

then.description: both_sides requires at least two evidence records — one per party.
